editorial_theme: The drive-in is back, and Friday night has four shows worth fighting traffic for. Summer in South County officially starts when Wuskenau Beach turns into a parking lot for Jaws.

headliner.name: Misquamicut Drive-In Season Opener — Jaws on the Beach
headliner.selection_reason: Opening night, summer ritual, unmistakably South County, pays off last week's teaser. The kind of single event where the room is the story.

lineup.name: Dirty Dozen Brass Band
lineup.description_raw: GRAMMY-winning New Orleans brass band — 45+ years, fusing brass with bebop, funk, R&B/soul. They invented 'musical gumbo.'
lineup.selection_reason: Major national act in a 400-seat room. Teased last week. Thursday gets a real reason to exist.

lineup.name: The Delightfully Respectful Celebration of the Nation's 50 States
lineup.description_raw: Dinner + presentation tour of all 50 states by Roberta Mudge Humble (RI Heritage Hall of Fame). BBQ chicken & pork, mac & cheese, Toll House cookies. $28 advance.
lineup.selection_reason: Civic-dinner weird, perfectly Westerly, $28 is a deal. The kind of thing readers tell friends about Monday.

lineup.name: Dance Night at the Knick — Eight to the Bar + UNITED Big Band Album Release
lineup.description_raw: Eight to the Bar (swing/boogie-woogie/Motown) shares the stage with the UNITED Big Band and Jazz Combo for an album release dance night. Dance floor is the point.
lineup.selection_reason: Teased last week. The Knick's birthday calendar continues. Dance Night = the room used the way it was meant to be used.

lineup.name: LaMP — Russ Lawton, Scott Metzger, Ray Paczkowski
lineup.description_raw: Jam trio with Trey Anastasio Band's rhythm section (Lawton, Paczkowski) plus Scott Metzger of JRAD/WOLF!. Phish-adjacent talent in a beach bar — does not normally happen.
lineup.selection_reason: Most surprising booking of the week. Will draw a regional jam-band crowd to a 200-cap beach bar. The Mist's deck on a Friday night with this lineup is a thing.

lineup.name: South County Art Association's 'Quality Seconds' Pottery & Art Sale
lineup.description_raw: Once-a-year discount sale of slightly-imperfect pottery and artwork from SCAA member artists. Collectors line up.
lineup.selection_reason: Annual + once-a-year + collector tribe. 2587 Kingstown Rd. Saturday morning move.

lineup.name: Mark Cutler & Men of Great Courage
lineup.description_raw: RI rock institution Mark Cutler — Schemers-era veteran — late-night set at the Pump House. $15 adv / $20 door.
lineup.selection_reason: Saturday-night Wakefield. Cutler is a hometown name with deep catalog. Pump House is on a run.

lineup.name: The Wailers — 50 Years of Positive Vibrations
lineup.description_raw: The Wailers play Bob Marley's Rastaman Vibration in full. Local opener. The Black Box is ~200 seats.
lineup.selection_reason: 50-year band in a 200-seat room. The Black Box turning into a reggae club for a night is a rare ticket.

lineup.name: RI Grown Farmers Market at Fishermen's Memorial
lineup.description_raw: Weekly Sunday market — produce, RI seafood, baked goods, flowers, dairy. Runs May through October.
lineup.selection_reason: The post-Wailers Sunday-morning move. The market is officially open for the season.

pvd_events.name: Newport Oyster & Chowder Festival
pvd_events.description_raw: Freshly shucked RI oysters and award-winning chowders from across the state's farms and kitchens, on the wharf, live music, cocktails.
pvd_events.selection_reason: Newport, not PVD, but the rare exception that earns it — Rhode Island's whole oyster industry on one wharf, perfect weather window.

spotlight_candidate.name: The Olympia Tea Room
spotlight_candidate.angle: Watch Hill institution since 1916. The room itself is the draw — old wood, the long bar, the way the light hits Bay Street. Open seasonally — comes back to life right around now. Ten minutes from the drive-in, perfect pre-Jaws dinner. Reservations smart on weekends.

deal_candidate.offer: $5 Off Any Advance Ticket This Week
deal_candidate.details: Mention 'South County Signal' for $5 off advance tickets to any of this week's three Pump House shows — Stacy Kendro Comedy (Sat 5/16, 7:30pm), Mark Cutler & Men of Great Courage (Sat 5/16, late), or Donna Byrne Quartet (Sun 5/17, 7pm). Continuation of the run from last week — they're booked solid.

signal_noise_candidates.question: What's the real first sign of summer in South County?
options: First show on the Ocean Mist deck
The first day you switch to iced coffee

signal_noise_candidates.question: Friday May 15 traffic jam — which one are you actually going to?
options: Eight to the Bar at the Knick
Van Morrison Moondance at the Courthouse

dropped.name: Pet Sounds Live! @ Odeum (Fri 5/15)
dropped.reason: Friday already over-stacked; classic-album-night format covered elsewhere recently

dropped.name: Van Morrison Moondance @ Courthouse (Fri 5/15)
dropped.reason: Same Friday-crowding issue; appears in Signal Noise poll instead

dropped.name: The Wallflowers @ Odeum (Sun 5/17)
dropped.reason: SOLD OUT — moved to Signal Noise / 'you snoozed' framing

dropped.name: Donna Byrne Quartet @ Pump House (Sun 5/17)
dropped.reason: Pump House already represented twice; surfaced via the Pump House deal

dropped.name: Roots Music Stomp Dylan @ UNITED Gallery (Wed 5/20)
dropped.reason: Late-week midweek timing; lineup already music-heavy

dropped.name: Machinal — Dance of Defiance @ URI (Thu 5/14)
dropped.reason: Thursday already covered by Dirty Dozen + 50 States dinner

dropped.name: Painting & Berry Bowl workshops
dropped.reason: Workshops with limited capacity — not lineup material

diversity_check.notes: Music is heavy at 4 but each pick is distinct (brass, swing/big-band, jam-trio, reggae, RI rock). Three Westerly events but spread across Thu/Fri/Sun. Friday intentionally over-stacked to drive the 'pick your Friday' Signal Noise debate.

teaser_seeds_for_next_week: Memorial Day Weekend — beach season official
Watch Hill Lighthouse opens for season
